title: Orange Pi BSP secure-access symbol audit
question: Do local BSP or public U-Boot sources name an SPC/TZMA/firewall/security register tied to SMHC0/SDMMC0 IDMAC memory access?
findings: Narrowed searches found A733 R-CCU TZMA clock entries and generic secure-only SMC/SPC/TZMA references, but no SDMMC0/SMHC0 access-control register or Linux-callable firewall setup.
The Orange Pi BSP SDMMC-relevant hits remain the already-tested mmc_store, mmc_mbus, and mmc_msi_lite clocks.
The public U-Boot A733 reference does not add an SDMMC0 secure/firewall handoff path; prior audits already showed it uses PIO for MMC data movement.
decision: No build and no patch. Do not test generic TZMA/SPC clocks as an SDMMC0 IDMAC fix without a source naming SMHC0 as a protected peripheral/master.
next_action: Keep the blocker unchanged: wait for maintainer/vendor feedback or a source-backed SDMMC0 access-control bit.
